page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-192GB is a memory-optimized virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of x86_64 system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities alongside weaker single-core execution. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated in the bottom 10% at 746.4, whereas the multi-core CPU performance reaches an average tier of 11309.02. Memory bandwidth benchmarks demonstrate stable throughput, with read-only large block transfers at 102.97 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 74.72 GB/sec. Geekbench testing yields a multi-core score of 8193.0 and a software compilation score of 15671.0, though text processing performance is weak at 1317.0.

The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff, as single-threaded tasks and text processing are relatively slow, but the massive 192.0 GB memory capacity provides high resource density for memory-bound workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a substantial memory footprint without the premium of dedicated CPU cores or bundled local storage. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications such as database caching, medium-scale Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that do not require sustained peak single-core CPU performance.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-192GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor, it provides 16 shared vCPUs and 192.0 GB of x86_64 memory, resulting in a high density of 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes no local storage and no GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, contrasted with poor single-core performance. This resource profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that can tolerate shared CPU allocation. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
